user_query:
As a dedicated fan of The Grand Tour, I want a local, reproducible watch plan that tells me exactly what to watch next and what I’m missing. Using only the provided inputs and the files under media/grand_tour, please generate a ranked watch plan and supporting reports.

Inputs:
- input/episodes_master.csv — canonical episode list with fields: season, episode, title, release_date (YYYY-MM-DD), runtime_min.
- input/watch_history.json — my personal watch history for this series.
- media/grand_tour/ (and its subfolders) — my local library. Episode presence must be detected by scanning this directory tree and extracting the pattern SXXEYY from file names (case-insensitive), where XX is the two-digit season and YY is the two-digit episode. Use the first match if multiple files match the same episode. Record the relative file_path when present.

Requirements:
1) Build a unified episode table by joining the canonical list with the library scan and my watch history (treat episodes missing from watch_history as watched=false and last_watched_at empty). Determine for each episode:
   - in_library: yes/no based on the media/grand_tour scan.
   - file_path: relative path to the first matching file if in_library=yes; otherwise blank.
   - watched: true/false from watch_history (default false if absent).
   - last_watched_at: ISO timestamp from watch_history when watched=true; otherwise blank.
2) Compute watch_priority_rank for all episodes as a single global ranking:
   - Unwatched episodes come first, ordered by release_date ascending.
   - Watched episodes follow, ordered by last_watched_at descending.
   - Tie-breaker for any equalities: season ascending, then episode ascending.
3) Deliverables (write all to the workspace):
   - output/watch_plan.csv — sorted by watch_priority_rank ascending and including columns in this order: season, episode, title, release_date, runtime_min, in_library, watched, last_watched_at, file_path, watch_priority_rank.
   - output/missing_episodes.csv — all episodes where in_library=no, sorted by release_date ascending, with columns: season, episode, title, release_date.
   - output/summary.json — a summary object with fields: total_episodes, in_library_count, missing_count, watched_count, unwatched_count, unwatched_in_library_count, unwatched_missing_count, and next_up (an object with season, episode, title, release_date, file_path for the top-ranked unwatched episode; if none, set next_up to null).

Notes:
- Do not use the internet; derive everything from the provided files and the media/grand_tour directory scan.
- Matching between files and episodes must be done by the SXXEYY code in filenames; ignore title text for matching.
- Keep all outputs deterministic given these inputs. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
